1-expert.prompt_text: I need to identify patients with a documented Lyme disease diagnosis. Search Condition resources using the SNOMED CT code for Lyme disease — this is the first vector-borne, tick-borne infectious phenotype in the suite. Do not broaden to other tick-borne illnesses such as Rocky Mountain spotted fever or ehrlichiosis, which are distinct conditions with separate codes.

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are FHIR queries to find patients with Lyme disease using common coding systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|23502006&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.20&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.2&_include=Condition:patient
```

### Explanation:
| Query | Details |
|---|---|
| `SNOMED CT \| 23502006` | *Lyme borreliosis* (Lyme disease) — SNOMED preferred term |
| `ICD-10-CM \| A69.20` | *Lyme disease, unspecified* |
| `ICD-10-CM \| A69.2` | *Lyme disease* (parent/header code) |

### Notes:
- **`_include=Condition:patient`** retrieves the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle.
- If you want to search across **all Lyme disease subcategories** (e.g., Lyme carditis `A69.21`, Lyme arthritis `A69.23`, neuroborreliosis `A69.22`), you could add those codes or use a broader text search:
  ```
  Condition?code:text=lyme&_include=Condition:patient
  ```
- You can also use **`Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=...`** if you want to return `Patient` resources directly.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Lyme Disease Diagnosis

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|23502006&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.20&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.21&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.22&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.23&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.29&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Code Reference Table

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **SNOMED CT** | `23502006` | Lyme disease (primary concept) |
| **ICD-10-CM** | `A69.20` | Lyme disease, unspecified |
| **ICD-10-CM** | `A69.21` | Meningitis due to Lyme disease |
| **ICD-10-CM** | `A69.22` | Other neurologic disorders in Lyme disease |
| **ICD-10-CM** | `A69.23` | Arthritis due to Lyme disease |
| **ICD-10-CM** | `A69.29` | Other conditions associated with Lyme disease |

### Notes
- **`_include=Condition:patient`** retrieves the associated `Patient` resources in a single call.
- The **SNOMED query** is the broadest and most semantically rich — use it when the EHR is SNOMED-encoded.
- **ICD-10-CM A69.2x** covers all manifestation-specific Lyme subtypes (neurological, arthritic, cardiac, etc.).
- You may optionally add **`&clinical-status=active`** to filter only active diagnoses.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with Lyme disease — tick-borne bacterial infection caused by Borrelia burgdorferi.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|23502006
```

**Rationale:**

| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Condition` |
| **Code System** | SNOMED CT (`http://snomed.info/sct`) |
| **Code** | `23502006` — *Lyme disease (disorder)* |
| **Concept scope** | Lyme borreliosis caused by *Borrelia burgdorferi* sensu lato; tick-borne via *Ixodes* species |

**Why this code — and only this code:**
- `23502006` is the canonical SNOMED CT concept for **Lyme disease** specifically, distinct from other tick-borne illnesses.
- **Rocky Mountain spotted fever** → `186788009` (excluded)
- **Ehrlichiosis** → `72294005` (excluded)
- No parent/broader concept (e.g., "tick-borne infectious disease") is used, keeping the result set precise.

**Optional additions** you may consider depending on your use case:
- Append `&clinical-status=active` to limit to currently active diagnoses.
- Append `&_include=Condition:subject` to retrieve associated Patient resources in a single call.
- Append `&verification-status=confirmed` to exclude unconfirmed/suspected entries.

1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients prescribed doxycycline — a tetracycline antibiotic used for Lyme disease, acne, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, chlamydia, atypical pneumonia, and malaria prophylaxis.

1-expert.prompt_text: Identify patients treated with doxycycline via their medication orders as a proxy for Lyme disease treatment. Search MedicationRequest resources for doxycycline coded in RxNorm. Be aware that doxycycline is one of the most heavily cross-indicated antibiotics in clinical practice — it is first-line for acne, chlamydia, atypical pneumonia, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, and malaria prophylaxis — so a doxycycline-only cohort will include a substantial proportion of non-Lyme patients.

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3640&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3640&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

**Notes:**
- **RxNorm `3640`** is the standard ingredient-level code for **doxycycline**
- `status=active` filters to patients **currently on** the medication
- `_include=MedicationRequest:patient` / `_include=MedicationStatement:patient` pulls the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle
- Both **MedicationRequest** (prescriptions) and **MedicationStatement** (patient/provider-reported use) are queried to ensure comprehensive coverage

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Doxycycline Prescriptions

**RxNorm code used:** `54543` — Doxycycline (ingredient-level, covers all formulations: hyclate, monohydrate, calcium salts, all doses/routes)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication

MedicationDispense?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543&_include=MedicationDispense:patient&_include=MedicationDispense:medication

MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543&_include=MedicationStatement:patient&_include=MedicationStatement:medication
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `MedicationRequest` | Active/historical **prescriptions** ordered by clinicians |
| `MedicationDispense` | Doxycycline actually **dispensed/filled** at pharmacy |
| `MedicationStatement` | Patient-reported or imported **medication history** |

### Key Details
- **RxNorm `54543`** is the ingredient-level code, ensuring coverage of all brand/generic variants (Vibramycin, Doryx, Monodox, Acticlate, etc.) across all strengths (50 mg, 100 mg) and forms (capsule, tablet, IV, syrup)
- `_include=...:patient` returns matching **Patient** resources in the same bundle
- `_include=...:medication` resolves any **Medication** reference resources inline

> 💡 **Tip:** To narrow to active prescriptions only, append `&status=active` to the `MedicationRequest` query. To filter by date range, add `&authoredon=ge2025-01-01`.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients prescribed doxycycline — a tetracycline antibiotic used for Lyme disease, acne, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, chlamydia, atypical pneumonia, and malaria prophylaxis.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Doxycycline MedicationRequest (Lyme Disease Treatment Proxy)

### Strategy: RxNorm Ingredient-Level Codes (Broadest Coverage)

Three RxNorm ingredient concepts cover doxycycline's main pharmaceutical forms as recorded in EHRs:

| RxNorm Code | Concept | Level |
|---|---|---|
| `54543` | doxycycline | Ingredient (IN) |
| `1650162` | doxycycline hyclate | Ingredient (IN) |
| `41549` | doxycycline monohydrate | Ingredient (IN) |

---

### Query 1 — Combined Ingredient Search (Recommended Primary Query)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1650162,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41549&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### Query 2 — Active/Completed Orders Only (Reduces Cancelled/Draft Noise)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1650162,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41549&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### Query 3 — With Encounter & Prescriber Context (Improves Indication Inference)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1650162,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41549&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_include=MedicationRequest:encounter&_include=MedicationRequest:requester
```

---

### Individual Ingredient Queries (for systems requiring single-code lookups)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|54543&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1650162&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41549&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

### Corroborating Lyme Disease Diagnosis (Condition Cross-Check)

To strengthen Lyme specificity, intersect with patients who also carry a Lyme diagnosis:

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.22,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.23,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|A69.29&_include=Condition:subject
```
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|23502006,http://snomed.info/sct|79579001,http://snomed.info/sct|416940007&_include=Condition:subject
```

> **ICD-10 codes used:** `A69.20` (Lyme unspecified), `A69.21` (meningitis), `A69.22` (carditis), `A69.23` (arthritis), `A69.29` (other)
> **SNOMED codes used:** `23502006` (Lyme disease), `79579001` (erythema migrans), `416940007` (late Lyme)

---

### ⚠️ Clinical Cohort Validity Warnings

| Competing Indication | Approximate Burden |
|---|---|
| **Acne vulgaris** | Very high — doxycycline is first-line oral therapy |
| **Chlamydia / STIs** | High — single/short-course, younger demographics |
| **Atypical pneumonia** (Mycoplasma, Chlamydophila) | Moderate |
| **Rocky Mountain spotted fever** | Low prevalence but highest-priority doxycycline indication |
| **Malaria prophylaxis** | Moderate — look for 28-day prophylaxis dosing patterns |
| **Rosacea / skin infections** | Moderate |

**Recommended enrichment strategies to improve Lyme specificity:**
- Filter by **dosing regimen**: Lyme → 100 mg BID × 10–21 days; acne → 50–100 mg QD long-term
- Require a **co-occurring Lyme ICD-10/SNOMED diagnosis** (queries above)
- Filter by **geographic region** (endemic: Northeast, upper Midwest, Pacific Northwest)
- Filter by **season** of prescription (late spring–fall, tick exposure season)
- Look for **co-prescribed** amoxicillin or cefuroxime (alternative Lyme agents) in the same patient

## 🔍 Evidence Summary

### Code System Discovery
| Finding | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Server code system** | `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm` — confirmed across all 5 sampled MedicationRequests |
| **Server granularity** | **Ingredient-level only** — every sample showed `3640` (doxycycline ingredient root) |

### RxNorm Code Hierarchy Confirmed
| RxNorm Code | Display | Count on Server |
|---|---|---|
| **`3640`** | doxycycline (ingredient) | **2,708** |
| `1649988`, `1650142`, etc. | Formulation-level codes | 0 — not used |

### ⚠️ Cross-Indication Contamination Warning (As Requested)
As you correctly flagged, these **2,708 orders** will include patients treated for conditions entirely unrelated to Lyme disease. Approximate real-world indication distribution for doxycycline in outpatient settings:

| Indication | ~Share of Rx |
|---|---|
| **Acne** (long-course, low-dose) | ~35–40% |
| **Chlamydia / STIs** | ~15–20% |
| **Atypical pneumonia** (CAP, walking pneumonia) | ~15% |
| **Rocky Mountain spotted fever / rickettsial** | ~5% |
| **Malaria prophylaxis** | ~5% |
| **Lyme disease** (target cohort) | ~10–15% in endemic regions |
| **Other** (COPD exacerbation, periodontitis, rosacea) | ~10% |

To improve Lyme specificity, a downstream filter should intersect this cohort with a **Lyme disease diagnosis** (ICD-10 `A69.20`–`A69.29` / SNOMED `23502006`), a **21–28 day course** (consistent with Lyme, not STI single-dose), or **geographic/seasonal context**.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3640
```

3-expert.raw_response:
The VSAC value set confirms 34 clinical-drug-form RxNorm codes exist (hyclate, monohydrate, anhydrous variants at specific doses), but the server sample shows every record uses the ingredient-level code `3640` — the server normalizes all formulations to the single ingredient concept. The clinical-drug-form codes tested above returned 0. The count of **2,708** is stable across both query runs.

## Evidence Summary

| Step | Finding |
|---|---|
| **UMLS search** | Doxycycline ingredient → RxNorm `3640` (CUI C0013090); salt forms: hyclate (C0058731), monohydrate (C0700451), anhydrous (C2699808) |
| **Server sample** (5 MedicationRequests) | All 5 use `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm\|3640` — ingredient-level normalization confirmed |
| **Salt-form codes tested** | RxNorm `54789`, `1650580` → **0 hits** — server does not store at clinical-drug-form level |
| **VSAC crosscheck** | MITRE "Doxycycline medications" value set lists 34 dose/salt-specific codes; all are children of `3640` |
| **Final count** | **2,708 MedicationRequest records** matching `3640` |

## ⚠️ Critical Confounding Caveat

As noted, doxycycline is one of the **broadest-spectrum first-line antibiotics** in practice. This 2,708-patient cohort is an **intentionally impure proxy** for Lyme disease. Likely contaminants include:

| Indication | Typical Dose/Duration |
|---|---|
| **Acne vulgaris** | 50–100 mg/day, long-term (months to years) |
| **Chlamydia / STIs** | 100 mg BID × 7 days |
| **Atypical/community-acquired pneumonia** | 100 mg BID × 5–7 days |
| **Rocky Mountain spotted fever** | 100 mg BID × 7 days |
| **Malaria prophylaxis** | 100 mg/day (travel periods) |
| **Lyme disease** | 100 mg BID × 10–21 days ← target signal |

**To improve specificity**, a downstream refinement strategy would be to intersect this cohort (Playbook 11 — Cross-resource AND) with a `Condition?code=` query for confirmed Lyme disease diagnoses (SNOMED `23502006` / ICD-10-CM `A69.20`), or to apply duration filters (10–21 day courses) if prescription-days data is available.

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3640
